annotations.label: Statistical mechanics

anchor.snippet: statistical mechanics is a mathematical framework that applies statistical methods and probability theory to large assemblies of microscopic entities

annotations.note: No grep hits for 'statistical mechanics' in Mathlib; the framework as a whole is not formalized.

annotations.label: Statistical ensemble

anchor.snippet: statistical mechanics introduces the statistical ensemble , which is a large collection of virtual, independent copies of the system in various states

annotations.note: No definition matching the physics notion of a statistical ensemble appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equilibrium ensembles and statistical equilibrium

anchor.snippet: These ensembles are known as equilibrium ensembles and their condition is known as statistical equilibrium

annotations.note: No formalization of equilibrium ensembles or statistical equilibrium is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sufficient condition for statistical equilibrium

anchor.snippet: A sufficient (but not necessary) condition for statistical equilibrium with an isolated system is that the probability distribution is a function only of conserved properties

annotations.note: This physics proposition about statistical equilibrium is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equal a priori probability postulate

anchor.snippet: For an isolated system with an exactly known energy and exactly known composition, the system can be found with equal probability in any microstate consistent with that knowledge

annotations.note: The equal a priori probability postulate is a physics postulate not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ergodic system

anchor.snippet: An ergodic system is one that evolves over time to explore "all accessible" states

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Ergodic` for ergodic measure-preserving maps, which is the underlying mathematical formalism, but not the physics notion of an evolving statistical-mechanical system.

annotations.label: Microcanonical ensemble

anchor.snippet: describes a system with a precisely given energy and fixed composition (precise number of particles)

annotations.note: The microcanonical ensemble is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Canonical ensemble

anchor.snippet: describes a system of fixed composition that is in thermal equilibrium with a heat bath of a precise temperature

annotations.note: No grep hits for 'canonical ensemble' in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Grand canonical ensemble

anchor.snippet: describes a system with non-fixed composition (uncertain particle numbers) that is in thermal and chemical equilibrium with a thermodynamic reservoir

annotations.note: The grand canonical ensemble is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gibbs theorem on equivalence of ensembles

anchor.snippet: all three of the ensembles listed above tend to give identical behaviour

annotations.note: Without the ensembles themselves formalized, Gibbs' equivalence theorem is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fluctuation–dissipation theorem

anchor.snippet: the response of a system when near equilibrium is precisely related to the fluctuations that occur when the system is in total equilibrium

annotations.note: No grep hits for 'fluctuation' relevant to this theorem in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Density operator in quantum statistical mechanics

anchor.snippet: a statistical ensemble (probability distribution over possible quantum states ) is described by a density operator S , which is a non-negative, self-adjoint , trace-class operator of trace 1 on the Hilbert space

annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of density operator, and grep finds no `Schatten`/`TraceClass` infrastructure on which to base it.
